def solve(n, m):
    if n > m:
        return 0
    L = m + 1
    # find min n^p for p >= L
    # try each bit position where we can "branch"
    best = float('inf')
    # go bit by bit
    bits = 30
    for i in range(bits, -1, -1):
        # consider setting bit i of p to be 1 when n's bit i is 0 (or vice versa)
        # making p > current prefix of L at bit i, then minimize remaining bits = match n
        mask = (1 << (i+1)) - 1
        prefix_L = L >> (i+1) << (i+1)  # upper bits of L
        # if upper bits of p match L, and bit i of p > bit i of L
        # then lower bits can be anything -> set to match n
        bit_L = (L >> i) & 1
        # set bit i of p to be > bit_L (i.e., 1 when bit_L=0)
        if bit_L == 0:
            p_candidate = prefix_L | (1 << i) | (n & ((1 << i) - 1))
            best = min(best, n ^ p_candidate)
    # also check p = L itself... already covered if we match all bits
    best = min(best, n ^ L) # not always valid but L >= L
    return best
